dataset_name: PRJEB18471 IBD gut microbiome dynamics profiling
study_name: Gut microbiome dynamics in a prospective IBD cohort
publication_title: Dynamics of the human gut microbiome in inflammatory bowel disease
disease: Crohn disease; ulcerative colitis; healthy controls
platform: Illumina sequencing; source library metadata indicate metagenomic PCR-based profiling
sample_size_samples: 555 longitudinal fecal samples reported by source/publication
potential_use_cases: Longitudinal gut microbiome dynamics; IBD flare/remission trajectories; CD/UC/control microbiome comparison
limitations: Sample-count mismatch across public sources: study text/publication report 555 samples while archive run tables expose more run/sample accessions; use source-reported cohort/sample counts until archive-level runs are reconciled
data_access_status: Public raw sequencing data; public metadata
notes: Candidate primary non-transcriptomic row from OQ132. Public source supports 118 individuals and 555 longitudinal fecal samples; archive run/sample accession counts should not be treated as unique biological sample counts without reconciliation. Do not label as shotgun metagenomics unless methods evidence is verified.
verification_notes: Prepared from data/verification/non_transcriptomic_top3_candidate_verification.csv. Preserve sample-count caveat: source-reported 555 longitudinal samples conflict with larger archive run/sample accession counts. Library metadata support conservative microbiome/metagenomic profiling language rather than an unverified shotgun label.
overlap_notes: Independent longitudinal IBD gut microbiome dynamics cohort. Count once as a microbiome/metagenomic-profiling cohort; do not inflate biological sample counts from archive run/accession totals until run-to-sample mapping is reconciled.

dataset_name: GSE112611 RISK pediatric Crohn blood DNA methylation
study_name: Blood-derived DNA methylation signatures of Crohn disease and intestinal inflammation severity
publication_title: Blood-derived DNA methylation signatures of Crohn's disease and severity of intestinal inflammation
potential_use_cases: Pediatric Crohn methylation biomarkers; blood-based inflammation severity signatures; RISK cohort epigenomics
limitations: Same broad RISK family as existing RISK transcriptomic rows DS005, DS040, and DS041; public metadata do not prove subject-level independence or overlap, so countability remains Unclear
data_access_status: Public raw and processed GEO data
notes: Candidate primary non-transcriptomic row from OQ132. GEO reports 402 peripheral blood EPIC methylation samples. Keep RISK overlap uncertainty explicit until a public or controlled subject crosswalk resolves DS005/DS040/DS041/GSE112611 relationships.
verification_notes: Prepared from data/verification/non_transcriptomic_top3_candidate_verification.csv. Countability intentionally left Unclear because this is a RISK-family row and public metadata do not resolve subject overlap with existing RISK transcriptomic rows.
overlap_notes: RISK pediatric Crohn blood methylation row. Same broad RISK family as DS005/GSE57945, DS040/GSE117993, and DS041/GSE93624, but public metadata do not provide a direct subject-ID crosswalk proving overlap or independence. Do not count as an independent RISK cohort without subject-level reconciliation.

dataset_name: ST003312/ST003313 Crohn flare serum and fecal metabolomics
study_name: Integrative serum and fecal metabolomics before Crohn disease flare
publication_title: Integrative analysis of metabolome and microbiome reveals alterations preceding a flare in patients with Crohn's disease
platform: LC-MS metabolomics; Metabolomics Workbench studies ST003312 and ST003313
study_design: Longitudinal or flare-prediction multi-specimen metabolomics study
sample_size_subjects: 80 CD patients and 43 controls reported by publication
sample_size_samples: 202 serum samples in ST003312; 294 feces samples in ST003313
potential_use_cases: Crohn flare prediction; serum-fecal metabolomics integration; microbiome-metabolome analysis
limitations: Treat ST003312 and ST003313 as one combined study-family row counted once; repository-embedded citation is less explicit than the PubMed title/abstract match
data_access_status: Public raw and processed Metabolomics Workbench data
notes: Candidate primary non-transcriptomic combined row from OQ132. Serum and feces Workbench studies should be represented as one row for the shared Crohn flare metabolomics study, not two independent cohorts.
verification_notes: Prepared from data/verification/non_transcriptomic_top3_candidate_verification.csv. ST003312 serum and ST003313 feces are combined into one proposed registry row and counted once; publication linkage is based on title/abstract/PubMed match rather than repository-embedded citation.
overlap_notes: Combined serum and fecal Crohn flare metabolomics study-family row. ST003312 and ST003313 should be searchable together and counted once; do not create separate independent cohort rows for the serum and feces accessions.
